posts.text:
🏆 ʻaʻaliʻi’s’dn’t’ve 🏆
The ʻaʻaliʻi’s would not have.

Quadruple contraction plus possessive ~ functionally a quintuple contraction.

Root word smuggles in three additional apostrophes, giving us a completely legal English seven apostrophe construction.

If we wanna go maximal glottal, native Hawaiian has words that are spelled with multiple okinas (apostrophes indicating glottal stops). There's a plant called the ʻaʻaliʻi, so a gardener could feasibly say something like: "those big pots are durable, but the ʻaʻaliʻi’s’dn’t’ve survived the trip."

reply_context.text:
if we're really careful, this can work with possessives as a base too. "My cat did something really stupid: someone else's'dn't've done that for sure."

Y'all can take a possessive, so y'all's'dn't've has to be legal. 

Quintuple contraction activated.

posts.text:
if we're really careful, this can work with possessives as a base too. "My cat did something really stupid: someone else's'dn't've done that for sure."

Y'all can take a possessive, so y'all's'dn't've has to be legal. 

Quintuple contraction activated.

reply_context.text: You can build this construction off of any proper name, which usually results in a quadruple contraction, but some proper names contain an internal apostrophe, so we've got some contenders along the lines of "D’Angelo’dn’t’ve". The next logical step is to explore words that have glottal stops.

posts.text: You can build this construction off of any proper name, which usually results in a quadruple contraction, but some proper names contain an internal apostrophe, so we've got some contenders along the lines of "D’Angelo’dn’t’ve". The next logical step is to explore words that have glottal stops.

posts.text: That said: let's assume their simulation was accurate/viable and the relevant spanning tree is three orders of magnitude denser today than it was then. Pretty sure the traversal here scales with O(logn), so the expected infection time would only have scaled 3x, so 1.5s.

reply_context.text: Scary stuff, but also that was 2004. Way fewer devices = shorter spanning tree. 510ms is approx time for an electric signal at ground level traveling unimpeded to circle the planet three times. Pushing the limits of physics here.
